$comment: Rawgentic project configuration schema v1. Used by /rawgentic:setup as reference when generating .rawgentic.json for a project.

project.$comment: Project identity and type. Consumed by all skills for project-type-specific behavior (application, library, infrastructure, etc.).
project.name: my-app

repo.$comment: Repository metadata. Consumed by all skills for gh CLI commands, branch creation, and PR targeting.

phaseExecutorTable.$comment: OPTIONAL versioned descriptor naming the project-owned phase-executor seat-table file (#445): a project-relative path to a routing-table JSON (phase_executor routing-table.schema.json) that is a complete replacement for the package default, never a merge overlay. Absent section = package default. The answered-defaults sentinel {"version": 1, "file": null} (#531) also resolves package-default — setup Step 2i stages it on decline/keep-defaults so key presence records the answer and the staleness nudge stops. This template is a reference example only — the executable validation contract lives in capabilities_lib (derive fails closed on any present-but-invalid section: version must be 1; file must be a relative path with no '..' — or the explicit null sentinel). Seeded/tweaked by /rawgentic:setup (#446).

telemetryAlerts.$comment: OPTIONAL per-project config for the #473 I3 seat-outcomes advisory alerts (AC-K5). ADVISORY only — no rule here can ever gate/block a run. Absent section (or the answered-defaults sentinel {"version": 1}) = all defaults. `enabled: false` disables evaluation (harvest still runs). windowSize (int 1..1000, default 30) + minSamples (int 1..windowSize, default 5) size the rolling baselines. thresholds: COUNT rules (fallback_fired, dispatch_failures) take false|non-negative-int (false disables that rule); TOGGLE rules (model_mismatch, parse_failure, seat_wall_time_p90, seat_cost_p90, review_findings_p90) take a bool. This template is a reference example only — the executable STRICT validation contract lives in hooks/seat_outcomes_lib.py (validate_telemetry_alerts; the `validate-config` CLI verb; setup Step 2j stages it, runtime load_thresholds fails open to these defaults). Seeded/tweaked by /rawgentic:setup Step 2j (#446).

$comment:techStack: Languages, frameworks, and infrastructure technologies. Consumed by setup, create-issue, implement-feature, optimize-perf, and update-deps for tooling selection.

testing.$comment: Test frameworks and commands. Consumed by implement-feature, fix-bug, refactor, optimize-perf, update-deps, update-docs, security-audit, and incident for test execution and CI verification.

database.$comment: Database connection and migration details. Consumed by implement-feature, fix-bug, optimize-perf, incident, and security-audit for queries, migrations, and health checks.
database.name: myapp_dev

$comment:services: Service definitions (ports, health checks, entry points). Consumed by create-issue, implement-feature, fix-bug, incident, security-audit, optimize-perf, and update-docs.

infrastructure.$comment: SSH hosts and Docker compose files. Consumed by implement-feature, fix-bug, refactor, incident, optimize-perf, update-deps, and security-audit for deployment and container operations.

deploy.$comment: Deployment method and command. Consumed by all SDLC skills (implement-feature, fix-bug, refactor, optimize-perf, update-deps, update-docs, security-audit) for merge-and-deploy steps.

security.$comment: Auth mechanisms, validation, and data channels. Primary consumer is security-audit; also used by create-issue and implement-feature for security-aware design.

ci.$comment: CI provider and workflow directory. Consumed by all SDLC skills for the CI verification gate after PR creation.

formatting.$comment: Code formatting tool and command. Consumed by implement-feature, fix-bug, and refactor for style compliance during code review.

documentation.$comment: Documentation file locations and format. Primary consumer is update-docs; also used by setup for initial doc detection.

$comment:protectionLevel: Per-project guard protection level preset. Controls which WAL and security guards are active. Values: sandbox (nothing blocked), standard (production data-transfer + orchestration + common code patterns), strict (all guards active). Default when missing: strict. Consumed by wal-guard and security-guard hooks.

guards.$comment: Explicit guard overrides. When present, overrides protectionLevel preset for the specified guard type. guards.wal lists active WAL guard rule names, guards.security lists active security guard rule names, guards.securityExcludePaths lists glob patterns for files excluded from security checks.

$comment:custom: Free-form project-specific metadata (e.g., security debt, feature flags). Any skill may read; security-audit checks for known debt items.
